bullets.text: Trump signals Iran cease-fire proposal is “significant” but insufficient; deadline tied to potential strikes on Iranian infrastructure raises escalation risk.
Iran’s 10-point proposal demands end to attacks and sanctions, highlighting sanctions relief as central bargaining chip for de-escalation.
Hormuz shipping traffic rises ahead of U.S. deadline, yet oil prices initially muted—market pricing implies limited disruption or ample spare supply/stock buffers.
Oil prices tick up as Trump reaffirms strike deadline, underscoring geopolitics-driven risk premium for crude and shipping insurance costs.
India resumes Iran oil and gas buying after 7 years, signaling limits to U.S.-aligned energy policy and potential sanctions-compliance tensions.
Broadcom expands chip/compute deals with Google and Anthropic, reinforcing custom silicon demand and supply-chain leverage amid AI infrastructure buildout.
Samsung forecasts record earnings on AI memory/chip demand, supporting thesis that AI capex is sustaining semiconductor cycle despite macro uncertainty.
AI data-center boom is “stress testing” insurers as private capital floods in, raising concerns about concentration, catastrophe exposure, and opaque private-market risk.
Jamie Dimon flags compounded risks from geopolitics, AI, and private markets—echoing growing worries about liquidity mismatches in private credit.
Trump administration finalizes better-than-feared Medicare Advantage rate, a near-term earnings tailwind for health insurers and a signal of moderated reimbursement pressure.
Move to scrap U.S. utility-bill aid amid high winter energy costs could worsen household energy burden and arrears, with knock-on effects for local economies and utilities.
Germany identifies alleged leaders of major ransomware gangs (REvil/GandCrab), showing increased cross-border law-enforcement pressure on cybercrime ecosystems.
Cryptography analysis argues practical quantum threats to widely used public-key systems likely remain years away, but migration planning is urgent due to long deployment cycles.
Salton Sea region’s lithium potential could strengthen U.S. battery supply chains, but scaling hinges on water, permitting, and extraction economics.
Artemis II’s lunar flyby advances deep-space operations experience, benefiting long-horizon science and space-industrial supply chains tied to NASA procurement.
